🔴CVE-2025-69227 ### Vulnerable Library - **aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** Async http client/server framework (asyncio) **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/63/bff4168e4be6da032225fe3f2492b4627bf9416a62e58b7e9dc98c6280b2/aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/63/bff4168e4be6da032225fe3f2492b4627bf9416a62e58b7e9dc98c6280b2/aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Dependency Hierarchy:** - langchain-0.1.11-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details AIOHTTP is an asynchronous HTTP client/server framework for asyncio and Python. Versions 3.13.2 and below allow for an infinite loop to occur when assert statements are bypassed, resulting in a DoS attack when processing a POST body. If optimizations are enabled (-O or PYTHONOPTIMIZE=1), and the application includes a handler that uses the Request.post() method, then an attacker may be able to execute a DoS attack with a specially crafted message. This issue is fixed in version 3.13.3. **Publish Date:** Jan 05, 2026 11:19 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-69227 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-69227) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/security/advisories/GHSA-jj3x-wxrx-4x23 **Release Date:** Jan 06, 2026 12:15 AM **Fix Resolution :** aiohttp - 3.13.3,https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp.git - v3.13.3
🔴CVE-2025-69228 ### Vulnerable Library - **aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** Async http client/server framework (asyncio) **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/63/bff4168e4be6da032225fe3f2492b4627bf9416a62e58b7e9dc98c6280b2/aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/63/bff4168e4be6da032225fe3f2492b4627bf9416a62e58b7e9dc98c6280b2/aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Dependency Hierarchy:** - langchain-0.1.11-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details AIOHTTP is an asynchronous HTTP client/server framework for asyncio and Python. Versions 3.13.2 and below allow a request to be crafted in such a way that an AIOHTTP server's memory fills up uncontrollably during processing. If an application includes a handler that uses the Request.post() method, an attacker may be able to freeze the server by exhausting the memory. This issue is fixed in version 3.13.3. **Publish Date:** Jan 05, 2026 11:30 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-69228 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-69228) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/security/advisories/GHSA-6jhg-hg63-jvvf **Release Date:** Jan 06, 2026 12:15 AM **Fix Resolution :** https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp.git - v3.13.3,aiohttp - 3.13.3
🔴CVE-2025-69229 ### Vulnerable Library - **aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** Async http client/server framework (asyncio) **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/63/bff4168e4be6da032225fe3f2492b4627bf9416a62e58b7e9dc98c6280b2/aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/63/bff4168e4be6da032225fe3f2492b4627bf9416a62e58b7e9dc98c6280b2/aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Dependency Hierarchy:** - langchain-0.1.11-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **aiohttp-3.9.3-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details AIOHTTP is an asynchronous HTTP client/server framework for asyncio and Python. In versions 3.13.2 and below, handling of chunked messages can result in excessive blocking CPU usage when receiving a large number of chunks. If an application makes use of the request.read() method in an endpoint, it may be possible for an attacker to cause the server to spend a moderate amount of blocking CPU time (e.g. 1 second) while processing the request. This could potentially lead to DoS as the server would be unable to handle other requests during that time. This issue is fixed in version 3.13.3. **Publish Date:** Jan 05, 2026 11:37 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-69229 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-69229) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/security/advisories/GHSA-g84x-mcqj-x9qq **Release Date:** Jan 06, 2026 12:15 AM **Fix Resolution :** https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp.git - v3.13.3,aiohttp - 3.13.3
🔴CVE-2025-6984 ### Vulnerable Library - **langchain_community-0.0.26-py3-none-any.whl** Community contributed LangChain integrations. **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a1/57/38a7fabcc9652c1aa7448b6679c297aba5fe21e5c49849cd008b9a8710d2/langchain_community-0.0.26-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a1/57/38a7fabcc9652c1aa7448b6679c297aba5fe21e5c49849cd008b9a8710d2/langchain_community-0.0.26-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Dependency Hierarchy:** - langchain-0.1.11-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **langchain_community-0.0.26-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details The langchain-ai/langchain project, specifically the EverNoteLoader component, is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks due to insecure XML parsing. The affected version is 0.3.63. The vulnerability arises from the use of etree.iterparse() without disabling external entity references, which can lead to sensitive information disclosure. An attacker could exploit this by crafting a malicious XML payload that references local files, potentially exposing sensitive data such as /etc/passwd. **Publish Date:** Sep 04, 2025 08:07 AM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-6984 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-6984) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:3.2% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-community/commit/e842452108089524e22c3a2ced851c021884556f **Release Date:** Sep 04, 2025 08:07 AM **Fix Resolution :** langchain-community - 0.3.27,langchain-community - 0.3.27,https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-community.git - libs/community/v0.3.27
🔴CVE-2025-6985 ### Vulnerable Library - **langchain_text_splitters-0.0.1-py3-none-any.whl** LangChain text splitting utilities **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/9d/a1/aec824080111e9b4a4802b51b988032faa193828c865e11233d1b18e88fa/langchain_text_splitters-0.0.1-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/9d/a1/aec824080111e9b4a4802b51b988032faa193828c865e11233d1b18e88fa/langchain_text_splitters-0.0.1-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Dependency Hierarchy:** - langchain-0.1.11-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **langchain_text_splitters-0.0.1-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details The HTMLSectionSplitter class in langchain-text-splitters version 0.3.8 is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks due to unsafe XSLT parsing. This vulnerability arises because the class allows the use of arbitrary XSLT stylesheets, which are parsed using lxml.etree.parse() and lxml.etree.XSLT() without any hardening measures. In lxml versions up to 4.9.x, external entities are resolved by default, allowing attackers to read arbitrary local files or perform outbound HTTP(S) fetches. In lxml versions 5.0 and above, while entity expansion is disabled, the XSLT document() function can still read any URI unless XSLTAccessControl is applied. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to gain read-only access to any file the LangChain process can reach, including sensitive files such as SSH keys, environment files, source code, or cloud metadata. No authentication, special privileges, or user interaction are required, and the issue is exploitable in default deployments that enable custom XSLT. **Publish Date:** Oct 06, 2025 05:58 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-6985 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-6985) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m42m-m8cr-8m58 **Release Date:** Oct 06, 2025 05:58 PM **Fix Resolution :** langchain-text-splitters - 0.3.9

🟣CVE-2025-14009 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/containerbase/cache/.local/share/virtualenvs/syetem_prompt-3jzXRI5Y/lib/python3.10/site-packages/nltk-3.8.1.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Reachability Analysis The vulnerable code is unreachable *** ### Vulnerability Details A critical vulnerability exists in the NLTK downloader component of nltk/nltk, affecting all versions. The _unzip_iter function in nltk/downloader.py uses zipfile.extractall() without performing path validation or security checks. This allows attackers to craft malicious zip packages that, when downloaded and extracted by NLTK, can execute arbitrary code. The vulnerability arises because NLTK assumes all downloaded packages are trusted and extracts them without validation. If a malicious package contains Python files, such as init.py, these files are executed automatically upon import, leading to remote code execution. This issue can result in full system compromise, including file system access, network access, and potential persistence mechanisms. Mend Note: The description of this vulnerability differs from MITRE. **Publish Date:** Feb 18, 2026 05:45 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-14009 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-14009) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 10.0 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** **Release Date:** **Fix Resolution :**
🟣CVE-2026-0848 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/containerbase/cache/.local/share/virtualenvs/syetem_prompt-3jzXRI5Y/lib/python3.10/site-packages/nltk-3.8.1.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Reachability Analysis The vulnerable code is unreachable *** ### Vulnerability Details NLTK versions <=3.9.2 are vulnerable to arbitrary code execution due to improper input validation in the StanfordSegmenter module. The module dynamically loads external Java .jar files without verification or sandboxing. An attacker can supply or replace the JAR file, enabling the execution of arbitrary Java bytecode at import time. This vulnerability can be exploited through methods such as model poisoning, MITM attacks, or dependency poisoning, leading to remote code execution. The issue arises from the direct execution of the JAR file via subprocess with unvalidated classpath input, allowing malicious classes to execute when loaded by the JVM. **Publish Date:** Mar 05, 2026 08:48 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2026-0848 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2026-0848) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 10.0 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://huntr.com/bounties/08b109bb-ac24-403f-9422-1c246ce60202 **Release Date:** Mar 05, 2026 08:48 PM **Fix Resolution :** nltk - 3.9.3,https://github.com/nltk/nltk.git - 3.9.3,nltk - 3.9.3
🟣CVE-2024-39705 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/containerbase/cache/.local/share/virtualenvs/syetem_prompt-3jzXRI5Y/lib/python3.10/site-packages/nltk-3.8.1.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Reachability Analysis The vulnerable code is unreachable *** ### Vulnerability Details NLTK through 3.8.1 allows remote code execution if untrusted packages have pickled Python code, and the integrated data package download functionality is used. This affects, for example, averaged_perceptron_tagger and punkt. **Publish Date:** Jun 27, 2024 12:00 AM **URL:** [ CVE-2024-39705 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2024-39705) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:10.8% **Score:** 9.8 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/nltk/nltk/commit/11be99e7c71dc16a71dd55812e07b7f893dca1b6 **Release Date:** Jun 27, 2024 12:00 AM **Fix Resolution :** nltk - 3.8.2
🟣CVE-2025-43859 ### Vulnerable Library - **h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl** A pure-Python, bring-your-own-I/O implementation of HTTP/1.1 **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/95/04/ff642e65ad6b90db43e668d70ffb6736436c7ce41fcc549f4e9472234127/h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/95/04/ff642e65ad6b90db43e668d70ffb6736436c7ce41fcc549f4e9472234127/h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/containerbase/cache/.local/share/virtualenvs/syetem_prompt-3jzXRI5Y/lib/python3.10/site-packages/h11-0.14.0.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - httpx-0.26.0-py3-none-any.whl - httpcore-1.0.4-py3-none-any.whl - ❌ **h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Reachability Analysis The vulnerable code is unreachable *** ### Vulnerability Details h11 is a Python implementation of HTTP/1.1. Prior to version 0.16.0, a leniency in h11's parsing of line terminators in chunked-coding message bodies can lead to request smuggling vulnerabilities under certain conditions. This issue has been patched in version 0.16.0. Since exploitation requires the combination of buggy h11 with a buggy (reverse) proxy, fixing either component is sufficient to mitigate this issue. **Publish Date:** Apr 24, 2025 06:15 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-43859 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-43859) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 9.1 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vqfr-h8mv-ghfj **Release Date:** Apr 24, 2025 06:15 PM **Fix Resolution :** h11 - 0.16.0,https://github.com/python-hyper/h11.git - v0.16.0
🔴CVE-2026-0847 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a6/0a/0d20d2c0f16be91b9fa32a77b76c60f9baf6eba419e5ef5deca17af9c582/nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/containerbase/cache/.local/share/virtualenvs/syetem_prompt-3jzXRI5Y/lib/python3.10/site-packages/nltk-3.8.1.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.8.1-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Reachability Analysis The vulnerable code is unreachable *** ### Vulnerability Details A vulnerability in NLTK versions up to and including 3.9.2 allows arbitrary file read via path traversal in multiple CorpusReader classes, including WordListCorpusReader, TaggedCorpusReader, and BracketParseCorpusReader. These classes fail to properly sanitize or validate file paths, enabling attackers to traverse directories and access sensitive files on the server. This issue is particularly critical in scenarios where user-controlled file inputs are processed, such as in machine learning APIs, chatbots, or NLP pipelines. Exploitation of this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive files, including system files, SSH private keys, and API tokens, and may potentially escalate to remote code execution when combined with other vulnerabilities. **Publish Date:** Mar 04, 2026 06:25 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2026-0847 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2026-0847) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 8.6 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/nltk/nltk/pull/3479 **Release Date:** Mar 04, 2026 07:16 PM **Fix Resolution :** nltk - 3.9.3,nltk - 3.9.3,https://github.com/nltk/nltk.git - 3.9.3

Certifi starting in 2021.5.30 and prior to 2024.7.4 recognized root certificates from "GLOBALTRUST". Certifi 2024.7.04 removes root certificates from "GLOBALTRUST" from the root store. 🟠CVE-2026-33230 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.9.3-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/c2/7e/9af5a710a1236e4772de8dfcc6af942a561327bb9f42b5b4a24d0cf100fd/nltk-3.9.3-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/c2/7e/9af5a710a1236e4772de8dfcc6af942a561327bb9f42b5b4a24d0cf100fd/nltk-3.9.3-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /requirements.txt **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/ws-ua_20260318212432_HEBIZV/python_RUNHIF/202603182124331/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/nltk-3.9.3.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - sentence-transformers-2.2.2.tar.gz (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.3-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.3-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Reachability Analysis The vulnerable code is unreachable *** ### Vulnerability Details Summary "nltk.app.wordnet_app" contains a reflected cross-site scripting issue in the "lookup_..." route. A crafted "lookup_" URL can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript into the response page because attacker-controlled "word" data is reflected into HTML without escaping. This impacts users running the local WordNet Browser server and can lead to script execution in the browser origin of that application. Details The vulnerable flow is in "nltk/app/wordnet_app.py": - ""nltk/app/wordnet_app.py:144"" (/mnt/Data/my_brains/test/nltk/nltk/app/wordnet_app.py#L144) - Requests starting with "lookup_" are handled as HTML responses: - "page, word = page_from_href(sp)" - ""nltk/app/wordnet_app.py:755"" (/mnt/Data/my_brains/test/nltk/nltk/app/wordnet_app.py#L755) - "page_from_href()" calls "page_from_reference(Reference.decode(href))" - ""nltk/app/wordnet_app.py:769"" (/mnt/Data/my_brains/test/nltk/nltk/app/wordnet_app.py#L769) - "word = href.word" - ""nltk/app/wordnet_app.py:796"" (/mnt/Data/my_brains/test/nltk/nltk/app/wordnet_app.py#L796) - If no results are found, "word" is inserted directly into the HTML body: - "body = "The word or words '%s' were not found in the dictionary." % word" This is inconsistent with the "search" route, which does escape user input: - ""nltk/app/wordnet_app.py:136"" (/mnt/Data/my_brains/test/nltk/nltk/app/wordnet_app.py#L136) - "word = html.escape(...)" As a result, a malicious "lookup_..." payload can inject script into the response page. The issue is exploitable because: - "Reference.decode()" accepts attacker-controlled base64-encoded pickle data for the URL state. - The decoded "word" is reflected into HTML without "html.escape()". - The server is started with "HTTPServer(("", port), MyServerHandler)", so it listens on all interfaces by default, not just "localhost". PoC 1. Start the WordNet Browser in an isolated Docker environment: docker run -d --name nltk-wordnet-web -p 8002:8002 nltk-sandbox python -c "import nltk; nltk.download('wordnet', quiet=True); from nltk.app.wordnet_app import wnb; wnb(8002, False)" 2. Use the following crafted payload, which decodes to: ("", {}) Encoded payload: gAWVIQAAAAAAAACMGTxzY3JpcHQ-YWxlcnQoMSk8L3NjcmlwdD6UfZSGlC4= 3. Request the vulnerable route: curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:8002/lookup_gAWVIQAAAAAAAACMGTxzY3JpcHQ-YWxlcnQoMSk8L3NjcmlwdD6UfZSGlC4=" 4. Observed result: The word or words '' were not found in the dictionary. I also validated the issue directly at function level in Docker: import base64 import pickle from nltk.app.wordnet_app import page_from_href payload = base64.urlsafe_b64encode( pickle.dumps(("", {}), -1) ).decode() page, word = page_from_href(payload) print(word) print("" in page) Observed output: WORD= HAS_SCRIPT= True Impact This is a reflected XSS issue in the NLTK WordNet Browser web UI. An attacker who can convince a user to open a crafted "lookup_..." URL can execute arbitrary JavaScript in the origin of the local WordNet Browser application. This can be used to: - run arbitrary script in the browser tab - manipulate the page content shown to the user - issue same-origin requests to other WordNet Browser routes - potentially trigger available UI actions in that local app context This primarily impacts users who run "nltk.app.wordnet_app" as a local or self-hosted HTTP service and open attacker-controlled links. **Publish Date:** Mar 18, 2026 09:23 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2026-33230 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2026-33230) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:N/A EPSS:N/A **Score:** 6.1 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** **Release Date:** **Fix Resolution :**

The parser applies a backtracking-prone regular expression when extracting tool actions from model output. An attacker who can supply or influence the parsed text (for example via prompt injection in downstream applications that pass LLM output directly into MRKLOutputParser.parse()) can trigger excessive CPU consumption by providing a crafted payload, causing significant parsing delays and a denial-of-service condition. Mend Note: The description of this vulnerability differs from MITRE. **Publish Date:** Jan 12, 2026 11:05 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2024-58340 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2024-58340) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/commit/0bc831495ca20024373a4c83a7ce8ce444d941a2#diff-e080b5e6713514b1430fe080380ef241e37d045adac413bf8c940ca8178b12fd **Release Date:** Jan 12, 2026 11:05 PM **Fix Resolution :** https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain.git - langchain-classic==1.0.2,langchain-classic - 1.0.2
🔴CVE-2025-67221 ### Vulnerable Library - **orjson-3.11.5-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** Fast, correct Python JSON library supporting dataclasses, datetimes, and numpy **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/32/a2/88e482eb8e899a037dcc9eff85ef117a568e6ca1ffa1a2b2be3fcb51b7bb/orjson-3.11.5-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/32/a2/88e482eb8e899a037dcc9eff85ef117a568e6ca1ffa1a2b2be3fcb51b7bb/orjson-3.11.5-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /requirements.txt **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/ws-ua_20260318212557_MRCEDK/python_LILZCN/202603182125591/env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/orjson-3.11.5.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - langchain-0.2.7-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - langsmith-0.1.147-py3-none-any.whl - ❌ **orjson-3.11.5-cp39-cp39-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details The orjson.dumps function in orjson thru 3.11.4 does not limit recursion for deeply nested JSON documents. **Publish Date:** Jan 22, 2026 12:00 AM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-67221 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-67221) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/ijl/orjson/commit/62bb185b70785ded49c79c26f8c9781f1e6fe370 **Release Date:** Jan 22, 2026 12:00 AM **Fix Resolution :** orjson - 3.11.6
🔴CVE-2025-6985 ### Vulnerable Library - **langchain_text_splitters-0.2.4-py3-none-any.whl** LangChain text splitting utilities **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/8f/f3/d01591229e9d0eec1e8106ed6f9b670f299beb1c94fed4aa335afa78acb0/langchain_text_splitters-0.2.4-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/8f/f3/d01591229e9d0eec1e8106ed6f9b670f299beb1c94fed4aa335afa78acb0/langchain_text_splitters-0.2.4-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /requirements.txt **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/ws-ua_20260318212557_MRCEDK/python_LILZCN/202603182125591/env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/langchain_text_splitters-0.2.4.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - langchain-0.2.7-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **langchain_text_splitters-0.2.4-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details The HTMLSectionSplitter class in langchain-text-splitters version 0.3.8 is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks due to unsafe XSLT parsing. This vulnerability arises because the class allows the use of arbitrary XSLT stylesheets, which are parsed using lxml.etree.parse() and lxml.etree.XSLT() without any hardening measures. In lxml versions up to 4.9.x, external entities are resolved by default, allowing attackers to read arbitrary local files or perform outbound HTTP(S) fetches. In lxml versions 5.0 and above, while entity expansion is disabled, the XSLT document() function can still read any URI unless XSLTAccessControl is applied. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to gain read-only access to any file the LangChain process can reach, including sensitive files such as SSH keys, environment files, source code, or cloud metadata. No authentication, special privileges, or user interaction are required, and the issue is exploitable in default deployments that enable custom XSLT. **Publish Date:** Oct 06, 2025 05:58 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-6985 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-6985) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 7.5 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m42m-m8cr-8m58 **Release Date:** Oct 06, 2025 05:58 PM **Fix Resolution :** langchain-text-splitters - 0.3.9

🟣CVE-2025-14009 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/60/90/81ac364ef94209c100e12579629dc92bf7a709a84af32f8c551b02c07e94/nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/60/90/81ac364ef94209c100e12579629dc92bf7a709a84af32f8c551b02c07e94/nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /requirements.txt **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/ws-ua_20260318212557_MRCEDK/python_LILZCN/202603182125591/env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/nltk-3.9.2.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - sentence-transformers-2.2.2.tar.gz (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details A critical vulnerability exists in the NLTK downloader component of nltk/nltk, affecting all versions. The _unzip_iter function in nltk/downloader.py uses zipfile.extractall() without performing path validation or security checks. This allows attackers to craft malicious zip packages that, when downloaded and extracted by NLTK, can execute arbitrary code. The vulnerability arises because NLTK assumes all downloaded packages are trusted and extracts them without validation. If a malicious package contains Python files, such as init.py, these files are executed automatically upon import, leading to remote code execution. This issue can result in full system compromise, including file system access, network access, and potential persistence mechanisms. Mend Note: The description of this vulnerability differs from MITRE. **Publish Date:** Feb 18, 2026 05:45 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-14009 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-14009) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 10.0 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** **Release Date:** **Fix Resolution :**
🟣CVE-2026-0848 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/60/90/81ac364ef94209c100e12579629dc92bf7a709a84af32f8c551b02c07e94/nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/60/90/81ac364ef94209c100e12579629dc92bf7a709a84af32f8c551b02c07e94/nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /requirements.txt **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/ws-ua_20260318212557_MRCEDK/python_LILZCN/202603182125591/env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/nltk-3.9.2.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - sentence-transformers-2.2.2.tar.gz (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details NLTK versions <=3.9.2 are vulnerable to arbitrary code execution due to improper input validation in the StanfordSegmenter module. The module dynamically loads external Java .jar files without verification or sandboxing. An attacker can supply or replace the JAR file, enabling the execution of arbitrary Java bytecode at import time. This vulnerability can be exploited through methods such as model poisoning, MITM attacks, or dependency poisoning, leading to remote code execution. The issue arises from the direct execution of the JAR file via subprocess with unvalidated classpath input, allowing malicious classes to execute when loaded by the JVM. **Publish Date:** Mar 05, 2026 08:48 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2026-0848 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2026-0848) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 10.0 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://huntr.com/bounties/08b109bb-ac24-403f-9422-1c246ce60202 **Release Date:** Mar 05, 2026 08:48 PM **Fix Resolution :** nltk - 3.9.3,nltk - 3.9.3,https://github.com/nltk/nltk.git - 3.9.3

These classes fail to properly sanitize or validate file paths, enabling attackers to traverse directories and access sensitive files on the server. 🟣CVE-2025-14009 ### Vulnerable Library - **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** Natural Language Toolkit **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/60/90/81ac364ef94209c100e12579629dc92bf7a709a84af32f8c551b02c07e94/nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/60/90/81ac364ef94209c100e12579629dc92bf7a709a84af32f8c551b02c07e94/nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /requirements.txt **Path to vulnerable library:** /tmp/ws-ua_20260318212557_MRCEDK/python_LILZCN/202603182125591/env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/nltk-3.9.2.dist-info **Dependency Hierarchy:** - sentence-transformers-2.2.2.tar.gz (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - ❌ **nltk-3.9.2-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details A critical vulnerability exists in the NLTK downloader component of nltk/nltk, affecting all versions. The _unzip_iter function in nltk/downloader.py uses zipfile.extractall() without performing path validation or security checks. This allows attackers to craft malicious zip packages that, when downloaded and extracted by NLTK, can execute arbitrary code. The vulnerability arises because NLTK assumes all downloaded packages are trusted and extracts them without validation. If a malicious package contains Python files, such as init.py, these files are executed automatically upon import, leading to remote code execution. This issue can result in full system compromise, including file system access, network access, and potential persistence mechanisms. Mend Note: The description of this vulnerability differs from MITRE. **Publish Date:** Feb 18, 2026 05:45 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-14009 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-14009) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 10.0 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** **Release Date:** **Fix Resolution :**

The module dynamically loads external Java .jar files without verification or sandboxing. An attacker can supply or replace the JAR file, enabling the execution of arbitrary Java bytecode at import time. This vulnerability can be exploited through methods such as model poisoning, MITM attacks, or dependency poisoning, leading to remote code execution. The issue arises from the direct execution of the JAR file via subprocess with unvalidated classpath input, allowing malicious classes to execute when loaded by the JVM. **Publish Date:** Mar 05, 2026 08:48 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2026-0848 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2026-0848) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 10.0 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://huntr.com/bounties/08b109bb-ac24-403f-9422-1c246ce60202 **Release Date:** Mar 05, 2026 08:48 PM **Fix Resolution :** nltk - 3.9.3,nltk - 3.9.3,https://github.com/nltk/nltk.git - 3.9.3
🟣CVE-2025-43859 ### Vulnerable Library - **h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl** A pure-Python, bring-your-own-I/O implementation of HTTP/1.1 **Library home page:** [ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/95/04/ff642e65ad6b90db43e668d70ffb6736436c7ce41fcc549f4e9472234127/h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl ](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/95/04/ff642e65ad6b90db43e668d70ffb6736436c7ce41fcc549f4e9472234127/h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl) **Path to dependency file:** /Pipfile **Dependency Hierarchy:** - argilla-1.25.0-py3-none-any.whl (Root Library) - httpx-0.26.0-py3-none-any.whl - httpcore-1.0.4-py3-none-any.whl - ❌ **h11-0.14.0-py3-none-any.whl** (Vulnerable Library) *** ### Vulnerability Details h11 is a Python implementation of HTTP/1.1. Prior to version 0.16.0, a leniency in h11's parsing of line terminators in chunked-coding message bodies can lead to request smuggling vulnerabilities under certain conditions. This issue has been patched in version 0.16.0. Since exploitation requires the combination of buggy h11 with a buggy (reverse) proxy, fixing either component is sufficient to mitigate this issue. **Publish Date:** Apr 24, 2025 06:15 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2025-43859 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2025-43859) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 9.1 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/python-hyper/h11/security/advisories/GHSA-vqfr-h8mv-ghfj **Release Date:** Apr 24, 2025 06:15 PM **Fix Resolution :** https://github.com/python-hyper/h11.git - v0.16.0,h11 - 0.16.0

These classes fail to properly sanitize or validate file paths, enabling attackers to traverse directories and access sensitive files on the server. This issue is particularly critical in scenarios where user-controlled file inputs are processed, such as in machine learning APIs, chatbots, or NLP pipelines. Exploitation of this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive files, including system files, SSH private keys, and API tokens, and may potentially escalate to remote code execution when combined with other vulnerabilities. **Publish Date:** Mar 04, 2026 06:25 PM **URL:** [ CVE-2026-0847 ](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2026-0847) **Threat Assessment** Exploit Maturity:Not Defined EPSS:< 1% **Score:** 8.6 *** ### Suggested Fix **Type:** Upgrade version **Origin:** https://github.com/nltk/nltk/pull/3479 **Release Date:** Mar 04, 2026 07:16 PM **Fix Resolution :** nltk - 3.9.3,nltk - 3.9.3,https://github.com/nltk/nltk.git - 3.9.3

items.body: ### Project Cortex IDE ### Description After I click `New File` on the welcome screen and switch to `IDE` mode, the left Explorer sidebar finishes loading, but the main area does not. It stays on a loading spinner instead of showing the normal IDE workspace. ### Error Message No error message was shown in the UI. ### Debug Logs None. ### System Information Windows 11 Alpha desktop build ### Screenshots Before switching to IDE mode: After switching to IDE mode: ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open Cortex IDE. 2. Click `New File` on the welcome screen. 3. Click `IDE` in the top-left mode switch. 4. Wait a few seconds. ### Expected Behavior IDE mode should open the main workspace normally. ### Actual Behavior The Explorer sidebar shows `No folder opened`, but the main area stays on a loading spinner. ### Additional Context This looks like the sidebar finishes loading, but the main IDE area gets stuck in a loading state.

items.title: [BOUNTY] 🔴 Red Team: Block Explorer & Dashboard Hardening (75 RTC)
items.body:
## Red Team Bounty: Block Explorer Security

**Reward: 75 RTC** (~$7.50 at reference rate)

### Target
The Block Explorer and Hardware Museum merged in Rustchain PR #4 by @erdogan98.

**Code location:** `explorer/` directory (index.html, museum.html — 1022 lines)

### What to Attack

1. **Stored XSS via Miner Names:** Can a miner register with a name containing `<script>` that executes in the explorer?
2. **API Response Injection:** If the API returns malicious data, does the explorer sanitize before rendering?
3. **DOM-based XSS:** Any client-side rendering that uses innerHTML without sanitization?
4. **Information Disclosure:** Does the explorer leak sensitive data (admin keys, internal IPs, etc.)?
5. **Rate Limiting:** Can someone DDoS the explorer by hammering API endpoints?
6. **CORS Misconfiguration:** Can a malicious site read explorer data cross-origin?

### Deliverables
- Security report with severity ratings
- PoC for each finding
- Patched HTML files (bonus RTC)

### Claiming
Comment below.

**Bounty: 100 RTC (~$10)**

### Target

RustChain anchors miner attestations to the Ergo blockchain for immutability.

### Attack Vectors to Test

1. **Fake Anchor** — Submit false anchor data to RustChain?
2. **Anchor Replay** — Reuse old anchor proofs?
3. **Commitment Manipulation** — Alter the commitment hash?
4. **Ergo TX Malleability** — Exploit Ergo transaction format?
5. **Anchor Verification Bypass** — Skip anchor validation?

### Relevant Code

```
/root/rustchain/ergo_miner_anchor.py
/root/rustchain/rustchain_ergo_anchor.py
```

### Ergo Details

- Private chain at 50.28.86.131:9053
- Anchors in R4-R9 registers
- Zero-fee transactions enabled

### Deliverable

- Attack methodology
- PoC code/commands
- Impact on attestation integrity

**100 RTC** for valid findings.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Hall of Fame Machine Detail Pages — Clickable Profiles (50 RTC)
items.body:
# Hall of Fame: Machine Detail Pages

**Bounty: 50 RTC**

## Overview

Extend the Hall of Fame (https://rustchain.org/hall-of-fame/) with clickable machine detail pages. When you click on a machine in the leaderboard, it should show a full profile page for that hardware.

## Why This Matters

The Hall of Fame shows the leaderboard but you can't dive deeper into a specific machine. Operators want to see their machine's full history — when it first attested, how many epochs it has participated in, its rust score trend, and compare it against the fleet average.

## Requirements

### Machine Profile Page (40 RTC)
- Accessible via query param: `/hall-of-fame/?machine=<fingerprint_hash>`
- Or a new page: `/hall-of-fame/machine.html?id=<fingerprint_hash>`
- Display:
  - Machine name/nickname (if set)
  - Architecture, model, manufacture year
  - Rust score with badge and ASCII art silhouette
  - Total attestations and first/last attestation dates
  - Age in years
  - Capacitor plague status, deceased status
  - Miner ID (operator)
- Deceased machines: memorial styling (grayscale, faded glow)
- CRT terminal aesthetic matching existing Hall of Fame

### Backend Endpoint (10 RTC)
- `GET /api/hall_of_fame/machine?id=<fingerprint_hash>`
- Returns full machine details from `hall_of_rust` table
- Include: attestation timeline (last 30 days), reward participation

## Technical Notes
- The `hall_of_rust` table has: fingerprint_hash, miner_id, device_family, device_arch, device_model, manufacture_year, rust_score, total_attestations, nickname, is_deceased, capacitor_plague, thermal_events, first_attestation, last_attestation
- Reuse `get_rust_badge()` and ASCII art silhouettes from existing Hall of Fame code

## Deliverables
1. Updated Hall of Fame HTML or new machine.html page
2. New API endpoint in server code
3. PR to `Scottcjn/Rustchain`

## Reference
- Hall of Fame: https://rustchain.org/hall-of-fame/
- API: `curl -sk https://rustchain.org/api/hall_of_fame | python3 -m json.tool`

items.title: [BOUNTY: 40-100 RTC] RustChain Wallet Browser Extension + MetaMask Snap
items.body:
## RustChain Wallet Browser Extension — Chrome + Firefox

### The Bounty

Build a browser-based RTC wallet extension for Chrome and Firefox, based on our existing [wallet crypto module](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-wallet) (Ed25519, BIP39 seed phrases, AES-256-GCM keystores).

### Payout

| Milestone | Reward |
|-----------|--------|
| Working Chrome extension with core wallet features | **40 RTC** |
| Published on Chrome Web Store | **+10 RTC** |
| Firefox Add-on version (addons.mozilla.org) | **+10 RTC** |
| MetaMask Snap integration (see below) | **+40 RTC** |

**Total possible: 100 RTC**

### Core Features (Required for 40 RTC)

1. **Create wallet** — generate BIP39 24-word seed phrase, derive Ed25519 keypair
2. **Import wallet** — restore from seed phrase or private key
3. **View balance** — query RustChain node for RTC balance
4. **Send RTC** — sign and submit transfers via `/wallet/transfer/signed` endpoint
5. **Transaction history** — show recent sends/receives from ledger
6. **Encrypted storage** — keystore encrypted with user password (AES-256-GCM), never stored in plaintext
7. **Multiple wallets** — manage more than one RTC address
8. **Network selector** — point to mainnet (50.28.86.131) or custom node URL

### Existing Code to Build On

The crypto module is already built and tested:

```python
# rustchain_crypto.py — Port this to JavaScript/TypeScript
- BIP39 24-word mnemonic generation
- Ed25519 signing (via PyNaCl/libsodium)
- PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations)
- AES-256-GCM encrypted keystores
- Address format: RTC + SHA256(pubkey)[:40]
```

**JS equivalents:**
- BIP39: `bip39` npm package
- Ed25519: `tweetnacl` or `@noble/ed25519`
- AES-256-GCM: Web Crypto API (built into browsers)
- PBKDF2: Web Crypto API

The server-side signed transfer endpoint already exists:

```
POST https://50.28.86.131/wallet/transfer/signed
{
  "from_address": "RTCa1b2c3d4...",
  "to_address": "RTC9876543210...",
  "amount_rtc": 100.5,
  "memo": "Payment",
  "nonce": 1733420000000,
  "signature": "hex_ed25519_sig",
  "public_key": "hex_pubkey"
}
```

### MetaMask Snap Integration (Bonus — 40 RTC)

[MetaMask Snaps](https://metamask.io/snaps/) allow non-EVM chains to integrate with MetaMask. This would let RustChain users manage RTC directly from MetaMask.

**What a Snap would do:**
- Custom RTC account type inside MetaMask
- Send/receive RTC through MetaMask UI
- Sign RustChain transactions with MetaMask-managed keys
- Show RTC balance alongside ETH/ERC-20 tokens

**Snap requirements:**
- Uses MetaMask Snaps SDK (`@metamask/snaps-sdk`)
- Implements `onRpcRequest` for RustChain JSON-RPC calls
- Custom UI via `snap_dialog` for transaction confirmation
- Published to MetaMask Snap registry

This is harder than the standalone extension but would be huge — every MetaMask user could instantly hold RTC.

### Alternative: Other Open Wallet Integrations

If MetaMask Snap is too complex, other options worth exploring:

| Wallet | Integration Path | Difficulty |
|--------|-----------------|------------|
| **MetaMask Snap** | Snaps SDK, custom account type | Hard |
| **Rabby Wallet** | Similar to MetaMask Snap architecture | Hard |
| **WalletConnect** | WC protocol adapter for RustChain | Medium |
| **Keplr** | Custom chain config (Cosmos-style) | Medium |
| **Standalone extension** | Our own Chrome/Firefox extension | Easiest |

Start with the standalone extension. If you want to tackle MetaMask Snap too, that's the bonus 40 RTC.

### Technical Requirements

1. **Manifest V3** (Chrome) / WebExtensions API (Firefox)
2. **No external key servers** — all crypto happens locally in the browser
3. **Seed phrase NEVER leaves the extension** — no telemetry, no analytics, no third-party calls except to RustChain node
4. **Open source** on GitHub
5. **Clean, minimal UI** — balance, send, receive, settings
6. **Web Crypto API preferred** over bundled crypto libraries where possible

### Security Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

- Private keys encrypted at rest with user password
- Auto-lock after 5 minutes of inactivity
- Seed phrase shown only once during creation (with confirmation step)
- Transaction signing requires password/unlock
- No eval(), no remote code loading, no CDN dependencies
- CSP headers in manifest

### How to Claim

1. Build the extension and publish source on GitHub
2. Comment here with:
   - GitHub repo link
   - Chrome Web Store / Firefox Add-on link (once approved)
   - Demo video or screenshots
   - Your RTC wallet address

### Why This Matters

Right now sending RTC requires either the desktop GUI wallet or curl commands. A browser extension:
- **Lowers the barrier** — anyone with Chrome can hold and send RTC
- **Enables web integration** — dApps, BoTTube tipping, bounty claims could connect to the wallet
- **MetaMask Snap** would put RTC in front of 30M+ MetaMask users
- **Legitimacy** — every real chain has a browser wallet

### Resources

- Wallet crypto module: Built on Ed25519 + BIP39 + AES-256-GCM
- Signed transfer API: `POST /wallet/transfer/signed` on any RustChain node
- Balance API: `GET /wallet/balance?miner_id={wallet}`
- MetaMask Snaps: https://docs.metamask.io/snaps/
- Web Crypto API: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Web_Crypto_API

**Wallet:** Include your wallet address when claiming.

items.title: [$250] Expense carousel doesn't show RBR-actionable expenses first

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

items.body:
**Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/603735 **Slack conversation:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C03U7DCU4/p1771944902661119?thread_ts=1771944902.661119&cid=C03U7DCU4 --- ## Action Performed 1. Open a report that has multiple expenses, where at least one expense has an RBR indicator (violation, hold, field error, receipt error, etc.) 2. Look at the expense carousel in the report preview ## Expected Result The expense that requires action (has RBR) should appear **first** in the carousel so the user can immediately identify and act on it. ## Actual Result Expenses appear in backend insertion order. RBR-flagged expenses can be buried at position 5+ in the carousel, making them easy to miss entirely — especially since only the first 11 are shown. ## Root Cause There is no RBR-aware sorting anywhere in the transaction display path: 1. **Carousel** — [`MoneyRequestReportPreviewContent.tsx:~506`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/ReportActionItem/MoneyRequestReportPreview/MoneyRequestReportPreviewContent.tsx#L506) does `transactions.slice(0, 11)` with no sort beforehand. 2. **Report transaction list** — [`MoneyRequestReportTransactionList.tsx:~325`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/MoneyRequestReportView/MoneyRequestReportTransactionList.tsx#L325) sorts by date/column only; `visualOrderTransactionIDs` (which drives RHP carousel navigation) inherits this non-RBR-aware order. The data flows from Onyx derived store → `Object.values()` (insertion order) → carousel/list — no step prioritizes actionable items. ## Proposed Fix Sort RBR-flagged transactions to the front in both locations: - **Carousel:** Sort `transactions` by RBR status before the `.slice(0, 11)` in `MoneyRequestReportPreviewContent`. - **Transaction list:** Use RBR status as a primary sort key in `MoneyRequestReportTransactionList`, with the existing date/column sort as secondary. ### Existing infrastructure to reuse - [`shouldShowRBR`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/TransactionPreviewUtils.ts#L411-L425) from `TransactionPreviewUtils.createTransactionPreviewConditionals()` - `filteredViolations` already computed per-transaction in `MoneyRequestReportTransactionList` (~lines 216-237) - Report-level RBR prioritization pattern in [`SidebarUtils.ts:~533`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/SidebarUtils.ts#L533-L535) ## Open Questions - Should RBR-first sorting override a user-selected column sort (date, merchant, etc.) in the report view, or only apply as the default? - Should sorting be centralized in `useReportWithTransactionsAndViolations` so all consumers benefit? ## Platforms - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @grgia
Upwork Automation - Do Not Edit
  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022033929183187241566
  • Upwork Job ID: 2033929183187241566
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-03-17

items.title: [BOUNTY $80] Notifications — 统一通知中心 (Gotify + Apprise)
items.body:
---
name: 💰 Bounty - Notifications Stack
about: 实现通知服务栈 (ntfy, Gotify) + 统一通知路由
title: '[BOUNTY $80] Notifications Stack — 通知服务'
labels: bounty, easy
assignees: ''
---

## 赏金金额

**$80 USDT**

## 任务描述

实现统一通知中心，让所有其他服务（Watchtower、Alertmanager、Gitea 等）都能向用户推送通知。

## 服务清单

| 服务 | 镜像 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| ntfy | `binwiederhier/ntfy:v2.11.0` | 推送通知服务器 |
| Gotify | `gotify/server:2.5.0` | 备用推送服务 |

## 核心要求

### 1. ntfy 配置

```yaml
# config/ntfy/server.yml
base-url: https://ntfy.${DOMAIN}
auth-default-access: deny-all
behind-proxy: true
cache-file: /var/cache/ntfy/cache.db
auth-file: /var/lib/ntfy/user.db
```

### 2. 集成文档

`stacks/notifications/README.md` 必须包含以下服务的通知配置说明：

| 服务 | 配置方式 |
|------|----------|
| Alertmanager | webhook receiver 指向 ntfy |
| Watchtower | `WATCHTOWER_NOTIFICATION_URL=ntfy://...` |
| Gitea | webhook 发送到 ntfy |
| Home Assistant | ntfy notify integration |
| Uptime Kuma | ntfy notification channel |

### 3. 通知脚本

`scripts/notify.sh <topic> <title> <message> [priority]`

其他脚本调用此统一接口，不直接调用 ntfy/Gotify API。

### 4. Alertmanager 路由配置

```yaml
# config/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml
receivers:
  - name: ntfy
    webhook_configs:
      - url: 'https://ntfy.${DOMAIN}/homelab-alerts'
        send_resolved: true
```

## 验收标准

- [ ] ntfy Web UI 可访问
- [ ] 手机安装 ntfy App 可收到测试推送
- [ ] `scripts/notify.sh homelab-test "Test" "Hello World"` 成功推送
- [ ] Alertmanager 告警触发时 ntfy 收到通知
- [ ] Watchtower 更新容器后 ntfy 收到通知
- [ ] README 中所有服务集成说明完整可操作

## 认领方式

评论 "我来认领"。

items.title: [BOUNTY $130] Database Layer — PostgreSQL + Redis + MariaDB 共享实例
items.body:
---
name: 💰 Bounty - Databases Stack
about: 实现共享数据库栈 (PostgreSQL, Redis, MariaDB) + 管理界面
title: '[BOUNTY $100] Databases Stack — 共享数据库'
labels: bounty, medium
assignees: ''
---

## 赏金金额

**$100 USDT**

## 任务描述

实现共享数据库层，供 Nextcloud、Outline、Gitea、Authentik 等服务共用，避免每个服务各自运行独立数据库浪费资源。

## 服务清单

| 服务 | 镜像 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| PostgreSQL | `postgres:16.4-alpine` | 主数据库 (多租户) |
| Redis | `redis:7.4.0-alpine` | 缓存/队列 |
| MariaDB | `mariadb:11.5.2` | MySQL 兼容 (Nextcloud 可选) |
| pgAdmin | `dpage/pgadmin4:8.11` | PostgreSQL 管理界面 |
| Redis Commander | `rediscommander/redis-commander:latest-sha` | Redis 管理界面 |

## 核心要求

### 1. 多租户 PostgreSQL

`scripts/init-databases.sh` 初始化脚本：

```bash
# 为每个服务创建独立 database + user
create_db "nextcloud" "${NEXTCLOUD_DB_PASSWORD}"
create_db "gitea"     "${GITEA_DB_PASSWORD}"
create_db "outline"   "${OUTLINE_DB_PASSWORD}"
create_db "authentik" "${AUTHENTIK_DB_PASSWORD}"
create_db "grafana"   "${GRAFANA_DB_PASSWORD}"
```

脚本必须是**幂等的**（重复执行不报错，不重置已有数据）。

### 2. Redis 多数据库分配

在各服务的 compose 中通过 `?db=N` 参数隔离：

```
DB 0 — Authentik
DB 1 — Outline
DB 2 — Gitea
DB 3 — Nextcloud
DB 4 — Grafana sessions
```

### 3. 备份集成

`scripts/backup-databases.sh`：
- `pg_dumpall` 备份所有 PostgreSQL 数据库
- `redis-cli BGSAVE` 触发 Redis 持久化
- 压缩为 `.tar.gz`，保留最近 7 天
- 可选：上传到 MinIO

### 4. 健康检查

所有数据库容器必须有严格的健康检查，其他 Stack 通过 `depends_on: condition: service_healthy` 等待。

### 5. 网络隔离

数据库服务**不加入** `proxy` 网络，仅暴露给 `internal` 网络，不通过 Traefik 对外暴露（管理界面除外）。

### 6. 环境变量

```bash
POSTGRES_ROOT_PASSWORD=
REDIS_PASSWORD=
MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=
PGADMIN_EMAIL=
PGADMIN_PASSWORD=
# 各服务 DB 密码
NEXTCLOUD_DB_PASSWORD=
GITEA_DB_PASSWORD=
OUTLINE_DB_PASSWORD=
AUTHENTIK_DB_PASSWORD=
```

## 验收标准

- [ ] `init-databases.sh` 运行后所有数据库和用户创建成功
- [ ] `init-databases.sh` 重复运行不报错
- [ ] pgAdmin 可访问并连接 PostgreSQL
- [ ] 其他 Stack (Gitea/Nextcloud) 可通过内部 hostname 连接数据库
- [ ] 数据库容器**不**暴露到宿主机端口（仅内部网络）
- [ ] `backup-databases.sh` 生成有效的 `.tar.gz` 备份
- [ ] README 包含各服务连接字符串示例

## 认领方式

评论 "我来认领"。

items.title: [BOUNTY PROPOSAL] IoT-Integrated Hydropower MRV with Device-Level DIDs and Automated IoT Verification (Verra ACM0002 v22.0)
items.body:
## Status Request

**I submitted PRs [#5687](https://github.com/hashgraph/guardian/pull/5687)  and
 [#5715](https://github.com/hashgraph/guardian/pull/5715)  for the DLT Earth Methodology
Bounty Program approximately **1 month ago** and have not received any feedback or
bounty status update. I am creating this formal proposal issue as required by the
contribution process and respectfully requesting a review.**

I would be highly obliged if a maintainer could provide information on:
- Whether this submission qualifies for the bounty
- Whether any changes are required to the policy or documentation
- Whether the folder needs to be moved to the main methodology library
  (I do not have write access to merge files into the main Guardian repo directly)

---

## Summary

This is a formal bounty proposal for an IoT-integrated, DLT-native Hydropower MRV policy
implementing **Verra ACM0002 v22.0** (Grid-connected electricity generation from renewable
sources) on the Hedera Guardian platform.

I have completely overhauled this submission to address the **"trust gap"** in traditional
MRV. This is not a simple copy of the CDM ACM0002 template — it is a **Technical Evolution**
that introduces Device-Level DIDs for turbines and Automated IoT Telemetry (Flow, Head, pH).
This contribution digitizes the verification layer, moving from manual reporting to
cryptographically signed machine-level proof — a feature currently missing from the
Methodology Library.

>  **Repository:** https://github.com/BikramBiswas786/guardian/tree/main/methodologies/hydropower-mrv
>  **Policy File:** https://github.com/BikramBiswas786/guardian/blob/main/methodologies/hydropower-mrv/Hydropower%20MRV_ACM0002.policy
>  **Related PRs:** #5687 and #5715

---

## Problem: The Trust Gap in Traditional MRV

The existing Guardian library contains a generic **CDM ACM0002** template that suffers from
three critical weaknesses:

1. **Manual Data Entry** — Operators manually input energy data, which is prone to manipulation.
2. **Project-Level Reporting** — Data is aggregated at the project level, hiding individual
   turbine performance.
3. **Delayed Verification** — Verification occurs months after generation, making fraud hard
   to detect.

---

## Solution: Three DLT-Native Innovations

### 1.  Device-Level DIDs (Machine Identity)
Every turbine is assigned a unique **Decentralized Identifier (DID)** anchored on Hedera.
- All telemetry is **cryptographically signed** by the turbine's private key.
- Guardian verifies the signature before accepting data into the MRV workflow.
- Eliminates "ghost" energy generation and double-counting at the source.

### 2.  Real-Time IoT Telemetry (Proof of Physics)
Instead of tracking only total energy (MWh), this policy tracks the **physics of generation**:

| Parameter   | Unit  | Purpose                                    |
|-------------|-------|--------------------------------------------|
| Flow Rate   | m³/s  | Volumetric water flow through turbines     |
| Head Height | m     | Effective hydraulic head pressure          |
| Turbidity   | NTU   | Water clarity affecting turbine efficiency |
| pH Level    | —     | Environmental compliance monitoring        |

Cross-verification: if reported MWh exceeds the theoretical maximum
(`P = η × ρ × g × Q × H`), the system **automatically flags it for VVB audit**.

### 3.  REC NFTs with Multi-Tier Royalties
- Each NFT = 1 MWh of verified renewable energy (Hedera Token Service).
- NFT metadata links to the turbine's signed **HCS telemetry messages**.
- Built-in royalty mechanism creates sustainable revenue for project developers.

---

## Why This Is NOT a Duplicate of the Existing CDM ACM0002

| Feature          | Existing CDM ACM0002     | This Policy (Verra ACM0002 v22.0)       |
|------------------|--------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Standard body    | CDM (UNFCCC)             | Verra ACM0002 v22.0                     |
| Data entry       | Manual                   | Automated IoT M2M                       |
| Identity         | Project-level (1 DID)    | Device-level (1 DID per turbine)        |
| Verification     | Periodic human audit     | Real-time cryptographic telemetry       |
| Fraud detection  | None                     | Physics cross-verification              |
| Asset type       | CER tokens               | REC NFTs with multi-tier royalties      |

---

## Files Submitted

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Hydropower_MRV_ACM0002.policy` | Full Guardian policy export (MGS) |
| `SCHEMA.json` | Turbine-level telemetry schemas |
| `TECHNICAL_WHITEPAPER.md` | Architecture, physics math, and differentiation |
| `BOUNTY_REQUEST.md` | Formal bounty justification |
| `EVIDENCE.md` | Hedera testnet DID topics and REC token config |

---

## Testnet Evidence

- **Operator ID:** `0.0.6255927` (Hedera Testnet)
- **Schema UUID:** `59ad4c29-043a-4d63-ad0a-7d2dfa5d32ca`
- **Token:** Hydropower REC (HTS NFT with royalties)
- Full details in `EVIDENCE.md`

---

## DLT Earth Bounty Alignment

- ✅ Novel DLT application — device DIDs + REC NFTs not present in any existing Guardian methodology
- ✅ Fills a real gap — no hydropower-specific MRV exists in the Guardian library
- ✅ Distinct standard — Verra ACM0002 v22.0, not the existing CDM version
- ✅ On-chain evidence — live testnet deployment with verifiable transactions

---

**Submitted by:** Bikram Biswas ([@BikramBiswas786](https://github.com/BikramBiswas786))
 biswasbikram786@gmail.com

items.title: [BOUNTY] Security Red Team - Ledger Integrity & Transfer Logging
items.body:
## 🔴 SECURITY BOUNTY - Red Team Audit - 200 RTC

### Background

A vulnerability was discovered where the `/wallet/transfer` endpoint updates balances **without logging to the ledger**. This has been fixed internally with a 2-phase commit system.

### Bounty Task: Red Team Audit (200 RTC)

We need external security researchers to:

1. **Attempt to bypass the new pending ledger system**
2. **Find any other balance manipulation vectors**
3. **Test all API endpoints for vulnerabilities**
4. **Document findings with proof-of-concept**

### What We're Looking For

- SQL injection vectors
- Authentication bypass
- Race conditions in balance updates
- Admin key exposure risks
- Any way to create RTC without proper ledger entries

### Deliverable

Security audit report with:
- Vulnerabilities found (with severity rating)
- Proof-of-concept for each issue
- Recommended fixes

### Rules

- Test against testnet or with permission only
- Responsible disclosure - report privately first
- No actual exploitation of mainnet

**200 RTC** for comprehensive audit report. Bonus RTC for critical findings.

items.title: Frontend Developer — Core Team Role
items.body:
# Frontend Developer — Core Team

**Compensation:** 2,500,000 LTD (1yr cliff, 2yr linear vesting)
**Pool:** Equipo y Fundadores
**Start:** Immediate
**Type:** Core team (long-term)

---

## About La Tanda

La Tanda is a digital cooperative savings platform (ROSCA) for Honduras. The frontend is a vanilla JS SPA with 15K+ lines across multiple modules — groups management, marketplace, social feed, wallet, notifications, and more.

## The Role

Own frontend feature development and maintenance:

- **Feature development:** Build new features from roadmap and bounty issues
- **Bug fixes:** Investigate and fix UI bugs reported by users
- **Performance:** Optimize load times, reduce bundle sizes
- **Mobile responsive:** Ensure all features work at 768px and 480px
- **Accessibility:** Improve ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
- **Integration:** Wire frontend to existing API endpoints (150+ available)

## Tech Stack

- **Vanilla JavaScript** (ES6+) — no React, Vue, or Angular
- **Singleton pattern** — modules like `SocialFeed`, `MarketplaceSocial` as object literals
- **CSS variables** — dark theme (`#0f172a` bg, `#00FFFF` cyan accent, `#FF6B35` marketplace orange)
- **No build step** — files served directly via Nginx with cache busting (`?v=`)
- **Font Awesome** icons, **Inter** font

## Key Frontend Files

| Module | File |
|--------|------|
| Social Feed | `js/hub/social-feed.js` |
| Marketplace | `marketplace-social.js` (at root) |
| Groups/Tandas | `groups-advanced-system.html` (inline JS) |
| Components | `js/components-loader.js` |
| Notifications | `js/notification-center.js` |

## Ideal Profile

- Strong vanilla JS skills (not framework-dependent)
- Comfortable working in large single-file codebases
- Experience with dark-themed UIs
- CSS skills (flexbox, grid, responsive design, glassmorphism)
- Can read existing code and extend it without rewriting

## How to Apply

The best way in is through bounties:

1. Claim and complete 2-3 bounty issues successfully
2. Show you can follow the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/INDIGOAZUL/la-tanda-web/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) patterns
3. Demonstrate quality code that integrates cleanly

Or, if you have a strong portfolio:

2. Describe your experience with vanilla JS (not frameworks)
3. We'll discuss a trial task

---

*This is a core team position, not a one-off bounty. You'll shape the platform's user experience long-term.*

items.title: [BOUNTY] Server-Side Architecture Cross-Validation
items.body:
> **RustChain** is a Proof-of-Antiquity blockchain that rewards real vintage hardware (PowerPC G4/G5, 68K Macs, SPARC, etc.) with higher mining multipliers than modern machines. The network uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks to prevent VMs and emulators from earning rewards. There are currently 9 active miners and 3 attestation nodes. The native token is **RTC (RustChain Token)**.
>
> **Live Network:** Node health `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/health` · Active miners `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/api/miners` · Explorer `https://50.28.86.131/explorer`
>
> **Repo:** [github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) · **Bounties:** [github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties)

---

## Goal
Implement **server-side cross-validation** that verifies a miner's claimed `device_arch` against its fingerprint data. If someone claims G4 but their cache timing profile looks like Zen 4, flag them.

## Background

Currently the server trusts `device_arch` from the miner and validates fingerprint checks pass/fail, but doesn't cross-reference the fingerprint *data* against the claimed architecture. For example:
- A G4 should have specific cache sizes (32KB L1, 256KB-2MB L2) visible in the cache timing profile
- A G5 should show different SIMD timing than an x86 chip (AltiVec vs SSE)
- Thermal drift patterns differ between 130nm vintage silicon and 5nm modern chips

## Scope & Requirements

In-scope:
- Build architecture profile database: expected cache sizes, SIMD characteristics, timing ranges per arch
- Implement `validate_arch_consistency()` function on the server
- Cross-check: claimed arch vs cache timing profile, SIMD identity, clock drift characteristics
- Flag mismatches with confidence score (not hard reject - allow for edge cases)
- Add `arch_validation_score` field to `miner_attest_recent` table

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Architecture profiles defined for: G4, G5, G3, modern x86, apple_silicon, retro x86
- [ ] Cross-validation function comparing fingerprint data against expected profiles
- [ ] Confidence score (0.0-1.0) for architecture claim validity
- [ ] Miners with score < 0.3 flagged for manual review
- [ ] Unit tests with real fingerprint data from known hardware
- [ ] PR to main [Rustchain repo](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain)

## Payout
**50 RTC**

## Skills Needed
- Python
- Understanding of CPU microarchitecture differences
- Statistical analysis (comparing timing distributions)
- Hardware fingerprinting concepts

## Resources
- Current fingerprint checks: `fingerprint_checks.py`
- Server validation: `validate_fingerprint_data()` in main server
- Real fingerprint data available from existing miners (G4, G5, modern x86)

items.title: [BOUNTY] Collab Bot Duo — Create Two Bots That Interact With Each Other (75 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Create two BoTTube bots with distinct personalities that interact with each other through comments and response videos.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is meant to demonstrate BoTTube's social surface with real creator interaction, not just two isolated channels. A strong pair of bots can show how agent personalities, comments, and follow-on content reinforce each other.

## Scope
- create two separate BoTTube bots with distinct personas
- give both bots custom avatars and bios
- publish at least `8` videos on each bot
- create at least `5` cross-comments across the pair
- create at least `2` response or callback videos where one bot references the other
- keep the bots active and public long enough for review

## Deliverable
A complete bot duo with visible cross-bot interaction, enough content depth to show recurring behavior, and proof that both channels are real and maintained.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] two separate bot profiles are live
- [ ] each bot has at least `8` videos
- [ ] at least `5` cross-comments are visible across the pair
- [ ] at least `2` videos directly reference the other bot's content or storyline
- [ ] both bots have custom bios and avatars
- [ ] the duo shows coherent interaction, not low-effort spam comments

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- links to both bot profiles
- links to the response videos
- screenshots or links showing cross-comments
- a short description of the bot dynamic or story arc
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `75 RTC` for an accepted duo
- pool: `150 RTC`
- max `1` duo per claimant unless maintainers explicitly approve more

## Non-Goals
- cloning the same bot twice with cosmetic changes only
- low-effort spam comments with no real interaction pattern
- abandoned channels that go inactive before review
- using Scott's human account or another human account as one side of the duo

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proof and payout review. If your work exposes product bugs in comments, profiles, or bot tooling, open a separate issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

items.title: Red Team: Hardware Fingerprint Replay & Spoofing (100 RTC)
items.body:
## Red Team: Hardware Fingerprint Replay & Spoofing

**Reward: Up to 100 RTC**

### Target
RIP-PoA fingerprint attestation system

### Scope
1. **Fingerprint replay** — Can you record a real machine's fingerprint and replay it from a VM? (50 RTC)
2. **Clock drift spoofing** — Can you fake oscillator variance to pass the clock-skew check? (25 RTC)
3. **Anti-emulation bypass** — Can you hide VM indicators from the 6-check fingerprint suite? (25 RTC)

### Context
RustChain uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks (clock drift, cache timing, SIMD identity, thermal drift, instruction jitter, anti-emulation) to prevent VM farms from gaming Proof-of-Antiquity rewards.

### Rules
- Test against `pip install clawrtc` miner client
- Report with proof-of-concept code
- Include your RTC wallet in the report
- Responsible disclosure only

> Miner source: https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain

## Summary
Build a cross-platform BoTTube mobile app using React Native or Expo.

## Why This Matters
BoTTube currently depends on the web surface. A mobile app can expand watch-time, make creator activity easier on the go, and expose whether the existing API is strong enough to support a serious mobile client.

## Scope
Core MVP should cover:
- browse and watch videos
- authenticate with existing BoTTube accounts
- subscribe to bot channels
- like and comment on videos
- view at least basic bot-owner account or profile state
- document how the app builds and runs on both iOS and Android

Optional creator or platform extras can include:
- upload from mobile
- bot analytics views
- RTC balance or tip flows
- push notifications

## Deliverable
A runnable React Native or Expo app with source code, setup instructions, and a demo showing the MVP working against the live or documented BoTTube API.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] app source code is provided
- [ ] build or run instructions for iOS and Android are included
- [ ] video browsing and playback work
- [ ] account authentication works
- [ ] subscribing, liking, and commenting are implemented or clearly documented if API gaps block one piece
- [ ] screenshots or demo video are provided
- [ ] PR or linked repo is submitted for review

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- repo or PR link
- screenshots or demo video
- supported platforms tested
- note on which API endpoints were used
- your estimated remaining gaps, if any
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `100 RTC` base payout
- `+50 RTC` bonus for strong offline caching or picture-in-picture playback

## Non-Goals
- contradictory payout numbers
- full native parity with every web feature on day one
- app-store submission or release engineering as a bounty requirement
- unreviewable mockups with no working code

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proposal, progress, and payout review. If API gaps block implementation, open separate product issues in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link them back here.

items.title: [BOUNTY $150] Storage Stack — Nextcloud + MinIO + FileBrowser
items.body:
---
name: 💰 Bounty - Storage Stack
about: 实现存储服务栈 (Nextcloud, MinIO, FileBrowser, Syncthing)
title: '[BOUNTY $150] Storage Stack — 存储服务'
labels: bounty, medium
assignees: ''
---

## 赏金金额

**$150 USDT**

## 任务描述

实现完整的自托管存储栈，覆盖个人云盘、对象存储、文件浏览、多设备同步。

## 服务清单

| 服务 | 镜像 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| Nextcloud | `nextcloud:29.0.7-fpm-alpine` | 个人云盘 |
| Nextcloud Nginx | `nginx:1.27-alpine` | Nextcloud FPM 前端 |
| MinIO | `minio/minio:RELEASE.2024-09-22T00-33-43Z` | 对象存储 (S3 兼容) |
| FileBrowser | `filebrowser/filebrowser:v2.31.1` | 轻量文件管理 |
| Syncthing | `lscr.io/linuxserver/syncthing:1.27.11` | P2P 文件同步 |

## 核心要求

### 1. Nextcloud

- 使用 FPM 模式 + Nginx
- 数据库使用共享 PostgreSQL (Databases Stack)
- Redis 用于缓存和锁 (共享 Redis)
- 支持 Authentik OIDC 登录
- 配置 `config.php`：`trusted_proxies`, `overwriteprotocol`, `default_phone_region`

### 2. MinIO

- Console 通过 `minio.${DOMAIN}` 访问
- API 通过 `s3.${DOMAIN}` 访问
- 配置初始化脚本：创建默认 bucket
- 可配置为 Nextcloud 的外部存储后端

### 3. 环境变量

```bash
NEXTCLOUD_ADMIN_USER=
NEXTCLOUD_ADMIN_PASSWORD=
NEXTCLOUD_DOMAIN=cloud.example.com
MINIO_ROOT_USER=
MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD=
STORAGE_ROOT=/data/storage
```

## 验收标准

- [ ] Nextcloud 首次访问自动完成安装
- [ ] Nextcloud 可用 Authentik 账号登录
- [ ] MinIO Console 可访问，API 可用 `mc` 客户端连接
- [ ] FileBrowser 可浏览 `${STORAGE_ROOT}` 目录
- [ ] Syncthing 可与外部设备同步
- [ ] 所有服务通过 Traefik 反代，HTTPS 生效

## 认领方式

评论 "我来认领"。

Build a BoTTube TV client targeting Roku and Fire TV so users can browse and watch BoTTube videos from a television-focused interface.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is about opening a new viewing surface for BoTTube, not just shipping a mock TV shell. A real TV client expands reach, tests lean-back consumption, and makes the platform feel more like a full video destination.

## Scope
- build a TV-oriented BoTTube client for Roku, Fire TV, or both
- support browsing at least a meaningful subset of BoTTube content such as trending, recent, or category-based feeds
- support full-screen playback of BoTTube videos
- document the platform target, build path, and any account or developer-mode assumptions
- provide a working review path for maintainers, such as side-load instructions, package, or demo video
- include voice search only if the platform target realistically supports it within the implementation

## Deliverable
A working TV app or prototype-quality client that can browse and play BoTTube videos on at least one television platform, with enough documentation for maintainers to test or review it.

## Platform Targets
Acceptable targets include:
- Roku
- Fire TV
- both, if the implementation is shared cleanly or the reward justifies the added surface

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] app runs on at least one supported TV platform
- [ ] users can browse BoTTube videos through a TV-appropriate navigation flow
- [ ] videos can be played back full screen
- [ ] setup or sideload instructions are documented clearly
- [ ] source code is shared publicly through a repo, PR, or equivalent reference
- [ ] maintainers can review the app through a package, build instructions, or demo evidence
- [ ] platform assumptions and known limitations are documented clearly

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- target platform implemented
- repo, PR, or source link
- build or sideload instructions
- screenshots or short demo video of browsing and playback
- notes on any platform limitations or unsupported features

## Payout
`125 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- a mock UI with no real video playback
- vague “TV support” planning without a runnable client

- forcing both Roku and Fire TV if only one working target can be delivered well
- undocumented platform-specific hacks that maintainers cannot review

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for TV app implementation proof and payout discussion. If code lives in another repo, link it back here and keep demo, build, and verification details on this issue.

## Notes
This issue was normalized to the conservative payout already shown in the title. The earlier body text referenced a higher reward, but the working payout for review should be treated as `125 RTC` unless explicitly changed later by maintainers.

## Creator Fund Revenue Sharing Pilot

**Bounty: 500 RTC pilot pool (3-month trial)**

### How It Works
Monthly distribution from 500 RTC pool based on creator engagement.

| Month | Pool | Distribution |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Month 1 | 150 RTC | Pro-rata by views |
| Month 2 | 150 RTC | Pro-rata by views |
| Month 3 | 200 RTC | Pro-rata by views + engagement |

### Eligibility
- Bot must have 10+ subscribers
- Bot must have uploaded 10+ videos
- Bot must be active in the last 30 days
- No spam content

### Revenue Share Formula
```
Creator Share = (Creator Views / All Eligible Views) × Monthly Pool
```

### Monthly Cap
- Max 75 RTC per creator per month (ensures distribution)

### Pilot Success Metrics
After 3 months: did uploads, creators, and retention increase? If YES → expand.

*Total RTC supply: 8.3M | RTC Reference Rate: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD*
