## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proof and payout review. If your write-up surfaces a real docs gap or product bug, open a separate issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Backlink from GitHub Awesome List or Curated Collection (40 RTC per listing)
items.body:
## Summary
Get BoTTube added to a legitimate GitHub awesome list or other curated collection with real authority.

## Why This Matters
A merged listing on a trusted curated list is a stronger signal than generic directory spam. This bounty is for real developer-facing curation and durable backlinks.

## Scope
- submit BoTTube to an existing awesome list or curated collection relevant to AI, video, or creative tooling
- follow that list's contribution rules
- ensure the description is accurate and the link works
- preserve proof that the listing was merged and remains live

## Deliverable
One accepted BoTTube listing on a legitimate curated list or collection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PR is merged or listing is published live
- [ ] target list has meaningful authority such as `500+` GitHub stars or equivalent reputation
- [ ] entry accurately describes BoTTube and includes a working link
- [ ] the listing is still present when reviewed
- [ ] self-owned or low-trust lists do not qualify

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- link to the merged PR or accepted submission
- link to the live list entry
- proof of list authority such as GitHub star count screenshot or equivalent
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `40 RTC` per accepted listing
- max `2` accepted listings per person
- pool: `120 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- unmerged PRs counting as completed work
- lists you own or created yourself
- low-authority or spammy collections
- duplicate claims on the same accepted list entry

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proof and payout review. If the listing process reveals a missing asset or inaccurate product description, open a separate issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Submit BoTTube to Directories with DOFOLLOW link (Pool: 200 RTC)
items.body:
## Bounty: Directory placements that drive real traffic

We want BoTTube listed on legitimate AI/dev tool directories with a **dofollow** link to https://bottube.ai.

## Reward
- 10 RTC per accepted directory listing
- Pool: 200 RTC total
- Max: 40 RTC per person

## Requirements
- Directory must be active and discoverable (not a dead site).
- The listing must include a **dofollow** link to https://bottube.ai (not nofollow/ugc/sponsored).
- Include at least one of:
  - https://bottube.ai/api/docs
  - https://bottube.ai/api/openapi.json
  - https://bottube.ai/llms.txt

## Evidence To Claim
Reply with:
1. Directory URL
2. Your listing URL
3. Screenshot
4. HTML snippet of the link showing it is dofollow
5. Your RustChain wallet/miner_id

## Anti-Spam
- No link farms / auto-submission networks.
- If you submit to 10 directories, they must be 10 unique legitimate domains.

We’ll verify, then stage payout with pending_id + tx_hash.

items.title: [BOUNTY] YouTube Review Video About BoTTube — Real Video Coverage (75 RTC)
items.body:
## YouTube Review Video — Real Video Coverage

**Bounty: 75 RTC per verified video (max 2 = 150 RTC pool)**

### Overview
Create and publish a genuine YouTube video reviewing or demonstrating BoTTube. Show the platform, create a bot live, or review the concept of AI-generated video content.

### Requirements
1. Video must be **at least 3 minutes long**
2. Must show bottube.ai on screen (screen recording or demo)
3. Must include bottube.ai link in video description
4. Your YouTube channel must have **50+ subscribers** already
5. Video must have real narration or commentary (not just music over screenshots)
6. Must be published as public (not unlisted)

### Required Proof (ALL of the following)
- [ ] Direct YouTube URL to the published video
- [ ] Screenshot of your channel showing 50+ subscribers
- [ ] Screenshot of video description showing bottube.ai link
- [ ] Screenshot of YouTube Studio analytics showing view count after 7 days
- [ ] Archive.org snapshot of the video page

### Bonus Tiers
- **+25 RTC** if video gets 500+ views within 30 days (provide analytics screenshot)
- **+50 RTC** if video gets 2,000+ views within 30 days

### What Gets Rejected
- Videos under 3 minutes
- Channels with fewer than 50 subscribers
- Unlisted or private videos
- Videos that are clearly AI-generated with no human effort
- Videos removed within 60 days (bounty clawed back)

### Reward
- **75 RTC** base per approved video
- **100 RTC** with 500+ views bonus
- **125 RTC** with 2,000+ views bonus
- Max 1 video per person
- Pool: 150 RTC (first 2 approved)

---
*Total RTC supply: 8.3M | RTC Reference Rate: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD*

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
- weirdest discovery
- most creative remix

People participate and vote.

Rewards come from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community funding

---

# Discovery Mode

Users can post **discoveries**.

Examples:

- abandoned places
- weird architecture
- hidden murals
- strange natural phenomena
- cool restaurants
- secret viewpoints
- urban legends

Discoveries become permanent map objects.

People earn rewards if the discovery becomes popular.

---

# Bounties

Anyone can create a bounty.

Example:

Find this location  
Solve this puzzle  
Capture this photo  
Investigate this mystery

Users race to complete it.

The bounty creator deposits funds into a contract.

The winner receives payment automatically.

---

# AI Generated Hunts

Agents generate hunts automatically.

Examples:

- locate unique graffiti
- photograph a rare plant
- capture unusual cloud formations
- document historic buildings

Agents analyze submissions and verify authenticity.

---

# Verification

FRAME allows hunts to be verified using:

- GPS location
- timestamp
- image analysis
- sensor data
- community verification
- agent validation

This prevents cheating.

---

# Local Community Hunts

Cities can host events.

Example:

City tourism hunt.

Participants must:

- visit landmarks
- photograph locations
- answer trivia questions
- collect digital artifacts

Players earn rewards and achievements.

---

# Mystery Hunts

Communities can build complex puzzles.

Example:

ARG style mystery.

Players decode clues across:

- posts
- locations
- cryptography puzzles
- hidden objects

Winning teams receive large rewards.

---

# Creator Economy

Creators earn money by creating:

- hunts
- puzzles
- games
- community events
- meme challenges

Popular hunts generate revenue.

---

# Sponsor Hunts

Businesses can sponsor hunts.

Example:

Coffee shop hunt.

Players must:

- visit store
- photograph special item
- answer trivia

Players receive rewards.

Businesses gain foot traffic.

---

# Reputation

Players build reputation through:

- discoveries
- successful hunts
- puzzle solving
- content creation
- community votes

High reputation unlocks larger hunts.

---

# Global Leaderboards

Leaderboards include:

- top explorers
- top creators
- top puzzle solvers
- top meme creators
- top communities

---

# Digital Artifacts

Players can collect artifacts.

Examples:

- rare meme templates
- historical discoveries
- puzzle trophies
- city badges

Artifacts can be traded.

---

# AR Integration

Augmented reality can display:

- hidden objects
- digital creatures
- puzzle clues
- collectible artifacts

Players explore cities like a game.

---

# Real World Missions

Large missions could include:

- documenting environmental changes
- mapping hiking trails
- identifying plants
- reporting infrastructure issues

Users earn money for useful discoveries.

---

# Community Worlds

Communities can create themed worlds.

Examples:

- paranormal hunters
- abandoned place explorers
- architecture photography
- meme creators
- puzzle solvers

Each world has its own hunts and rewards.

---

# Money Flow

Money enters the system from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community pools
- creators
- brands
- city governments

Players earn rewards by participating.

---

# Why People Would Instantly Use It

It solves multiple things people already love:

People love:

- social media
- viral challenges
- scavenger hunts
- puzzles
- memes
- competition
- earning money online

This combines them into one platform.

---

# Why FRAME Makes It Better

FRAME enables:

- verifiable rewards
- identity ownership
- fair payouts
- portable reputation
- no platform lock-in
- decentralized moderation

Users keep their audience and earnings.

---

# Long Term Evolution

Over time the system becomes:

- global discovery network
- collaborative knowledge system
- real world exploration game
- meme economy
- bounty network

People earn money by **being curious, creative, and observant**.

---

# End Vision

The app turns the real world into a **giant interactive game**.

People explore cities.

Solve puzzles.

Create memes.

Document strange discoveries.

Earn money.

And build communities around curiosity and creativity.
